site.name: Bitfield
site.description: Public docs for Bitfield, the plug-in runtime and database for products that need to stay fast as they grow.

pages.title: Bitfield Docs
pages.description: Start here if you want to understand Bitfield and use the public Runtime Kit surface.
pages.summary: The public entry point for Bitfield docs.

pages.title: Quickstart
pages.description: Get a key, add Runtime Kit, read data, and send a request.
pages.summary: The shortest path from a Bitfield key to the first app-facing Runtime Kit calls.

pages.title: Get your key
pages.description: How keys, checkout, account access, and the first device activation fit together.
pages.summary: Explains what happens when you get a Bitfield key and activate your first device.

pages.title: What is Bitfield?
pages.description: A plain-English explanation of Bitfield as a database and runtime.
pages.summary: Explains Bitfield without assuming the reader already knows database or runtime vocabulary.

pages.title: Database and runtime
pages.description: What Bitfield stores, what it runs, and why those two pieces belong together.
pages.summary: Defines the public database and runtime model.

pages.title: Active devices
pages.description: What counts as an active device and how revoking or replacing a device works.
pages.summary: Defines active devices for pricing, keys, and account management.

pages.title: JavaScript Runtime Kit
pages.description: Use the app-facing JavaScript and React surface.
pages.summary: Shows the public JavaScript Runtime Kit shape without exposing owner machinery.

pages.title: Packages
pages.description: How Runtime Kit packages declare what should be stored and run.
pages.summary: Documents the public package boundary file and package-set idea.

pages.title: Local state
pages.description: What lives under the Bitfield local state folder and what each area means.
pages.summary: Documents customer-visible local state at a high level.

pages.title: How Bitfield is this fast
pages.description: A plain-English speed proof with category boundaries.
pages.summary: Explains warm local reads, memory mapping, content addresses, and non-claims.

pages.title: Benchmark ledger
pages.description: Public benchmark claims with category boundaries and non-claims.
pages.summary: Lists public speed claims with the work category attached.

pages.title: Runtime Kit API
pages.description: The current public Runtime Kit values.

pages.title: Changelog
pages.summary: The public change log for docs and customer-visible product behavior.
